*   **Table 1: Operating conditions of Single Sector Combustor**

    |                    | VALUE             | UNIT    |
    | ---------          |:-------------:    | :----  |
    | upper calorific value  | 10.19          | kWh/m³        |
    | lower calorific value  | 9.191           | kWh/m³        |
    | density at 1.013 bar, 0°C | 0.7939         | kg/m³         |
    | relative density     | 0.614           | -           |
    | Wobbe index          | 13.005          | kWh/m³        |

*   **Table 2: Combustion related physico-chemical properties of natural gas**

 *Units are referenced against values in Table 1.

*   **Operating conditions**
| Operating condition | Cold    | Hot     | Hot     |
| ------------------ |:-------:|:-------:|:-------:|
| combustor pressure | 2 bar | 2 bar | 10 bar |
| burner pressure drop | 2.75%  | 2.75%  | 2.75%  |
| plenum temperature | 280 K  | 650 K  | 650 K  |
| secondary air temp. | 280 K  | 670 K | 680 K  |
| AFR  | no fuel  |  25.5 | 25.5 |
| burner pressure drop | 0.055bar | 0.055bar | 0.275bar |
| burner air mass flow | 52.3 g/s  | 35.7 g/s | 181 g/s |
| natural gas mass flow | 3.4 g/s air | 1.4 g/s | 7.1 g/s |
| prim. zone film cooling air | 11.2 g/s | 5.9 g/s | 26.4 g/s |
| mixing air mass flow | 18.0 g/s | 22.3 g/s | 112 g/s |
| mixing zone film cooling | 28 g/s | 12 g/s | 48 g/s |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 2: Longitudinal section of the burner**
    *   Components and labels:
        *   Outer swirler (60 deg)
        *   Inner swirler (60 deg)
        * measurement plane 1 mm from burner outlet
        *   Air plenum
        *   Natural Gas Inlet
        * outlet of cooling air for primary
            zone windows

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirl angle: 60°
*   Slot dimensions: 1.5 x 1 mm (36 slots)
*   Distance of slots ending before the burner outlet: 1 mm
*   Flame temperatures: 700K
*   Pressure: 4 bar
*   Combustor Pressure: 2 bar, 10 bar
*   Plenum Temperature: 280 K, 650 K
*   Secondary Air Temp.: 280 K,

title: 06___non_intrusive_flowfield_temeperature_and_species_measurments_on_a_generic_a
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   z = 2 to 34 mm (Mapping primary zone)
*   lambda=532 nm (Wavelength of Nd:YAG lasers)
*   -35 < y < 35 mm (Measurements in y-z planes)

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 3: Chemical Composition of the Used Natural Gas**

    | Species             | Mol-%    |
    | ------------------- | -------- |
    | Carbon dioxide CO2  | 0.8385   |
    | Nitrogen N2         | 9.1064   |
    | Oxygen O2 + Argon Ar | < 0.01   |
    | Methane CH4         | 87.874   |
    | Ethane C2H6         | 1.7695   |
    | Propane C3H8        | 0.3083   |
    | n-Butane C4H10      | 0.0426   |
    | i-Butane C4H10      | 0.0303   |
    | n-Pentane C5H12     | 0.0057   |
    | i-Pentane C5H12     | 0.0067   |
    | Neo-Pentane C5H12   | 0.0019   |
    | C6 and higher       | 0.0161   |

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Flow velocity measurement uncertainty: 2-4 m/s (single shot), 0.1-0.2 m/s (mean velocity, 1000 shots)
*   Laser pulse energy: 1 J
*   Laser focus diameter: 0.5 mm
*   CARS probe volume length: 3.2 mm
*   z = 10, 24, 18, 22, 26 mm (Measurement location heights)
*   x = -10, 0, 10 mm (around jets)
*   z = 85, 95, 105, 115, 125 mm (heights)
*   T = 1560 K (Measured mean temperature)
*   Accuracy for lab burner measurements at atmospheric conditions: 2% (mean temperature), 2-3% (rms(T))
*   Accuracy for lab burner measurements at 10 bar: 3% (mean temperature), 4-5% (rms(T))
*   150 um diameter and 1.5 mm length (Dimension of the measuring volume)
*    1 m/s (Detection limit)
*    Plane at x = -10, 0, 10 mm (Around the jets)
*     z = 85, 95, 105, 115 and 125 mm between -35 < y < 35 mm. (Plane heights)

    *   Curves:
        *   u (axial velocity)
        *   v (radial velocity)
        *   w (tangential velocity)
    *   Key values: None explicitly labelled.

    *   Axes:
        *   x: phi [deg] angle from -10 to 10
        *   y: velocity [m/s] from 0 to 20
    *   Curves:
        *   u ax, mean (mean axial velocity)
        *   u ax, rms (RMS axial velocity)
    *   Key values: None explicitly labelled.
*   **Figure 6:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: x [mm] from -15 to 15
        *   y: z [mm] from 90 to 110
    *   Contours and Vectors:  Velocity magnitude (v [m/s]) indicated by color contours ranging from -90 to 10. Velocity direction indicated by vectors. Reference vector shown at 10 m/s. Circle denotes Jet hole contour.
*   **Figure 7:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x: x [mm] from -15 to 15
        *   y: z [mm] from 90 to 110
    *   Contours and Vectors:  Velocity magnitude (v [m/s]) indicated by color contours ranging from -90 to 10. Velocity direction indicated by vectors. Reference vector shown at 10 m/s. Circle denotes Jet hole contour.

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

*   **Figures 6 & 7:**
    *   Components: Jet hole, velocity vectors (arrows), color contours representing velocity magnitude.
    *   Labels: x, z axes, v[m/s] color scale.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   LDA measurement location: 1 mm from burner outlet.
*   Light sheet thickness: 1 mm.
*   Mixing jet port location: y = 49 mm.
*   Frequency peak: 1.7 KHz.
*   Burner annulus radius: r = 12 mm.
*   Peak RMS radial velocity observed at r = 0 mm with value of 25 m/s.
*   Wake of the fuel ring at r = 10 mm.
*   Velocity measurement plane: 1 mm from central hole.

        *   y axis: z [mm] from 80 to 120, in steps of 10

        *   Contour plot representing the magnitude of velocity.
    *   Key Values:

        *   Arrow indicating 100 m/s reference vector.
*    **Figure 15: Mean temperatures in the plane of the jets at x = 0 mm at 2 bar**
    *   Axes:
        *   x axis: y [mm] from -20 to 20, in steps of about 7
        *   y axis: z [mm] from 80 to 130, in steps of about 10
    *   Curves:
        *   Contour plot representing the mean temperature.
    *   Key Values:
        *  Color scale bar representing <T>/K from 650 to 1650.
*   The top figures contain identical coordinate axes and colormaps

**3. Tables:**

NO_DIAGRAMS

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   x = 0 mm (Position of the jet plane)
*   2 bar (Pressure condition)
*   z = 95 mm (Line along which mixing of cold jets and hot primary air is easily followed)
*   z = 85 mm (Measurement upstream of the jet axis)
*   -5 mm < y < 5 mm
*   |V| (magnitude of velocity)
*   <T>/K (Mean Temperature)
*   1650 K (maximum recorded <T>)
*    650 K (minimum recorded <T>)
